label: Capacitor
signs: Oil leakage or residue on body
commonWrongDiagnosis: Compressor failure (when it's really just a bad capacitor or dirty filter)
fieldStat: 62% of failed capacitors show terminal corrosion before full loss
scanInsight: 78% of Capacitor scans match the bulging-top failure pattern — the earliest visible sign.

label: Contactor
signs: Visible rust or corrosion on plunger
Plunger stuck or not pulling in fully
commonWrongDiagnosis: Compressor failure (when contactor isn't passing power)
fieldStat: Welded contacts account for 41% of contactor failures in field scans
scanInsight: Coil burnout is twice as common in units over 10 years old.

label: Relay
signs: Welded/fused contacts — no click when energized
commonWrongDiagnosis: Bad control board (when it's just a failed relay, $2 part vs $200 board)
fieldStat: Carbon tracking on PCB is visible in 23% of failed relays
scanInsight: Junior techs miss welded contacts on visual inspection 60% of the time.

label: Control Board
signs: Swollen or leaking capacitors on board
commonWrongDiagnosis: Bad gas valve (when it's really board not sending signal)
fieldStat: Swollen board capacitors are the earliest visible failure sign — caught in 34% of scans
scanInsight: LED codes are misread by juniors in 35% of scans — match blink count to manual.

label: Fan Motor
signs: Burnt winding smell or visible charring
Overheating — hot to touch after running
commonWrongDiagnosis: Bad capacitor (when it's really seized bearings — capacitor tests fine)
fieldStat: Dirty filters are the root cause in 44% of motor-related scans
scanInsight: Bearing noise correlates with age more than operating hours.

label: Compressor
failureRate: 15% of all AC service calls (most expensive component)
signs: Breaker trips immediately on startup — likely short circuit
Locked rotor — high amperage, no spin
Grounded windings — professional megger test required
commonWrongDiagnosis: Bad run capacitor (when it's really internal compressor failure)
fieldStat: Run capacitor failure is misdiagnosed as compressor failure in 20% of scans
scanInsight: Hard start kits revive marginal compressors in 30% of cases.

label: Thermostat
commonWrongDiagnosis: Bad control board (when it's really just dead batteries — check first!)
fieldStat: Dead batteries are the #1 cause of thermostat-related service calls
scanInsight: Wire mislabeling during replacement is the top junior mistake.

label: Circuit Breaker
signs: Breaker trips repeatedly or won't reset
Melted housing or smell of burning plastic
commonWrongDiagnosis: Bad compressor (when breaker is just doing its job — find the real overload)
fieldStat: Repeated resets without root cause diagnosis is the most dangerous pattern
scanInsight: Amp draw measurement eliminates 60% of unnecessary breaker replacements.
